_meta.description: Templates for generating domestic Latvian boxing events on the calendar. These shapes are read by the event generation system to procedurally create events — real cities, dates, and fighter fields are assigned at generation time. Event generation logic is not implemented here. hostCityRotation on the national championship drives actual host city rotation; the current index is tracked in world state and advanced after each running of the event.

eventTemplates.label: Club Tournament
eventTemplates.description: Small club-run amateur tournament sanctioned by the LBF. Held in the host club's home gym or a local sports hall. Weight classes offered vary by entrant demand — the organiser confirms which classes run based on registrations received. Serves as the primary entry point into competitive boxing for new fighters in the city.

eventTemplates.label: Regional Open
eventTemplates.description: Multi-club regional tournament drawing from across a Latvian region. Hosted by a regional sports complex or the largest gym in the region. Attracts fighters from multiple cities seeking to build their ranking ahead of the national championship. A strong result here is the standard marker the LBF weighs when evaluating national championship applications.

eventTemplates.label: Latvian National Championship
eventTemplates.description: Annual national championship, always held in November. Crowns a Latvian National Champion in all 10 weight classes. The host city rotates on a fixed schedule tracked in world state — the engine reads the current rotation index, selects the host city, then increments the index. The LBF selects a venue appropriate to the host city from the eligible venue pool. Entry is application-based: coaches apply, the federation evaluates and publishes the accepted list before the event is finalised on the calendar.
